storyInfo: Caster personally leads her troops to Kjerag, letting all know of Enciodes's 'exploitative, dictatorial, wicked deeds'. Elder Adso encounters Enya's party atop Mount Karlan. The two of them debate religion, heading deeper into Karlan together to seek the truth.

attributes.content: So according to you, that Duke Caster has had her spies embedded in Kjerag for a long, long time. And it's all to prepare for a future annexation?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: But how is she going to do that?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: To be more precise... this is my educated guess based on what I've learned in Kjerag.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: So out of all the Victorians sneaking around Kjerag, you happen to be the only actual reporter who's after truth and righteousness and suchlike?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Well, you don't have to be so rude about it!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: My name's Alberta. If you're familiar with the Victorian newsprint business, you would've heard that name in conjunction with many articles about the truth that were deemed 'unprintable'.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: I take pride in my work, no matter what they say.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Hmph...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I'll believe you for now, little Miss Reporter, but this doesn't mean you're completely free of suspicion.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I've had my guards rip out the spies your people sent after the explosion.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: If you're telling the truth, then take us to your crew's station. I've got a lot of things I'd like to ask the agents you mentioned.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: You wouldn't refuse me, would you?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: But—
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Move!
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: Degenbrecher grabs Ratatos and the reporter, pulling them into the back room as a stun grenade from who-knows-where goes off in the interrogation room.
In the distance, a Browntail guard who was unable to get out of the way in time screams. A throwing knife has pierced his heart.
Dammit!
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Degenbrecher pokes her body out the doorway, hiding back behind the door as another knife flies towards her. She watches it tremble after it embeds itself in the door frame.
These knives are the ones used by the Trilby Ashers.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: You two stay hidden here. I'll go confirm the scale of the raid and their strategy.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: As she finishes speaking, she suddenly draws her twin weapons, knocking two knives thrown from the shadows out of the air before springing forward to attack. The dust she kicks up forces the reporter to squint.
You—
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: What the hell's going on? How do the Trilby Ashers know about this place?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Did you leak it to them? But I've been watching you the whole time, that can't be...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I didn't! I even checked over all my things, and made sure nobody was following me.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Look out!
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: The room shakes violently. The reporter pulls at Ratatos's sleeve, moving her to the other side of the room as fragments of the damaged ceiling smash into the floor where they just were.
Then how do you explain all this?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: ...Wait! There's still one place I haven't checked.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: She picks up the camera hanging around her neck, removes the lens, presses down on the shutter, and lifts up the reflector. A faint red light glows within.
She pulls out the suspicious component. A miniature tracking device.
So that's where it was hiding...
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Another explosion, and thick smoke slowly seeps into the room the two are hiding in. Degenbrecher emerges from the smoke, her breathing somewhat ragged.
Three Trilby Ashers with this first group. I fended them off for now, but the reinforcements they called for are already on their way here.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: I will keep you safe, Ratatos, but we need to leave.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: Let's go to the Vine-Bead Court. There's a mountain trail nearby that leads to one of the secret passages in.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: They won't dare act rashly against the Court. It'll buy us some time.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Wait, I need to come with you.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Since the Trilby Ashers will keep chasing us, I'll make a record of all their assassination attempts in Kjerag and compile them together with everything they've done so far. I'll pass them to a local friend to get the word out.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: This will expose all of Victoria's dirty deeds to the public.
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: I can't let myself be taken in by them!
attributes.name: Alberta

attributes.content: Enough babbling, I still need to find Enciodes for a joint questioning.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: You'd be coming with us even if you didn't want to.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: ...Yes, Ratatos is alive. The three of them have entered the Vine-Bead Court's northern gate.
attributes.name: Trilby Asher

attributes.content: The escaped reporter is with her, and the renowned Black Knight is guarding them.
attributes.name: Trilby Asher

attributes.content: What orders from Her Grace?
attributes.name: Trilby Asher

attributes.content: ...Wait.
attributes.name: Trilby Asher

attributes.content: ...Did you just say that Her Grace will personally be visiting Kjerag?
attributes.name: Trilby Asher

attributes.text: \n\nand to Enciodes Silverash, son of the former Chairman and my kinswoman Elizabeth
\n\nThis is an open letter, within which is written all the wrongdoings of Enciodes and Karlan Trade both.
\n\nWith this letter I shall formally protest, protest all the grievances that Victoria and Kjerag share in suffering from, that were wholly avoidable.

attributes.content: Chairman Edelweiss! I'm glad you're here!
attributes.name: Karlan Trade Employee

attributes.content: What is it? Slow down.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I-It's a letter by the Duke of Caster. The contents have already been disseminated to the public.
attributes.name: Karlan Trade Employee

attributes.content: It's all about Kjerag... Mostly Karlan Trade.
attributes.name: Karlan Trade Employee

attributes.content: What did she write?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: About how we're not acting in good faith, and Sir Enciodes—
attributes.name: Karlan Trade Employee

attributes.content: Give it here.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Notify all members of the Board that we are to have an immediate meeting at HQ.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Tell everyone to remain calm.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.text: \n\nEnciodes's first offense, and the source of all this tragedy, was to seek benefit from Columbia, brute-forcing the carving-out of a great pit and the construction of an observation station atop Mount Karlan.
\n\nIn the wake of it, Catastrophe descended on Kjerag. This much is undeniable.
Amidst the terror and disarray, he committed his second offense. The humanitarian aid sent by Victoria was instead pocketed by him in the name of negotiation.
\n\nTo legitimize this shameless act, Enciodes went on the attack against Victoria, leveling baseless accusations and slander against Victorian tourists and expatriates. This was his third offense.

\n\nHis ultimate act of toying with the heart of the public was also his fourth offense. He colluded with Ratatos to fake the Browntail matriarch's death, and through duplicity, lead Clan Browntail into hostilities with Victoria.

attributes.content: '...Victoria also has proof that Ratatos is hidden within the Vine-Bead Court. Those citizens who doubt this simply need examine the premises, though his guilty heart has led him to station his private army there.'
attributes.name: Doubtful Local

attributes.content: This is... all a bit much.
attributes.name: Doubtful Local

attributes.content: Hey, when I was suspicious of Enciodes earlier, you guys shouted me down like I was a conspiracy theorist. But it all makes sense now, doesn't it?
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: Since when? The whole letter's just Victoria's say-so.
attributes.name: Determined Local

attributes.content: The fifth offense explained it, didn't it? Enciodes told Ratatos to kidnap that Victorian reporter and hid her in the Court. Can't be a lie with all those details, can it?
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: And the sixth offense says in order to control the Court, he arrested all the clerics who wouldn't obey him. That matches.
attributes.name: Doubtful Local

attributes.content: The Court invited us all up the mountain for blessings, but Degenbrecher had some men stop us.
attributes.name: Doubtful Local

attributes.content: What about the seventh, then? It's obvious that Victoria wanted to get a bigger slice of the pie, but Karlan Trade refused them.
attributes.name: Determined Local

attributes.content: You're a real idiot, you know that? It's proof that Enciodes has gone and betrayed Kjerag.
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: Victoria's made a fair point about Karlan Trade going against all their contracts, and now we're the ones stuck with the bill thanks to Enciodes's stupid decisions!
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: So you think we should do what Victoria says? They want Karlan Trade to stick with the old mining agreement, and put Enciodes on trial!
attributes.name: Determined Local

attributes.content: They said they'd do a joint trial with the Vine-Bead Court, it'll be fine. Enciodes would be lucky if he just got banished for his crimes.
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: The only crime he's done is letting you run your mouth in this rebuilt pub!
attributes.name: Determined Local

attributes.content: So he's been doing all the good, while it's always someone else doing the sinning? Wow, what a coincidence!
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: ...I don't understand any of this, but I know it's always us who have to suffer for it.
attributes.name: Doubtful Local

attributes.content: C'mon man, trust in Sir Enciodes, he's helped me out before—
attributes.name: Determined Local

attributes.content: What the hell was that?
attributes.name: Discontent Local

attributes.content: O-Outside...
attributes.name: Barkeeper

attributes.content: ...I heard a lot of troubling talk on the way here.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Please tell me you've been doing more than admiring the snow.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I shouldn't have asked.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Is everything that happened still as you expected?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: ...If I said yes, would you believe me?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I would.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Because we're not unprepared. During the recent growth window, Karlan Trade—
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I am afraid it is you who is the optimistic one this time, Gnosis.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You know that, no matter how many plans and contingencies we make, things will inevitably exceed our expectations.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: From the three clans, the Vine-Bead Court, and stretching out to all the people of Kjerag, Victoria's methods are plain to see.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As someone who always bets every chip he has, you were never afraid of risk.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Thus, all this is all I deserve.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Dark clouds cover the twilight sky. The steel behemoths of Victoria trample across the white snowfields.
Caster's fleet...
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I've heard this fleet was the one that drove the devils out of Victoria during the Battle of Londinium.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: After the battle concluded, Caster took the time to restore its flagship, so that her Glory may live on.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: How many upon this great land can boast of the might to stand against this force?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: ...We chose to walk the more difficult road. It is time we meet the end of it.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Whew—
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Why are all of Kjerag's mountains so hard to climb...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Just bear it a little longer, Elena. We need to avoid the Vine-Bead Court, and this is the only road that does so.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: A Rift Cloudbeast clambers up onto Elena's shoulder out of nowhere before jumping back into Joanna's embrace, letting out a high-pitched cry.
Heh, it's laughing at you.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: Laugh all you want... I've done more exercise today then the past twenty-something years I've been alive...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Enya looks at the crestfallen Elena, letting out a light chuckle as she turns the corner.
She sees a simple camp by the side of the road, not far from where they stand.
There are many clerics of the Vine-Bead Court warming themselves by the fire. Amidst the crowd, Enya picks out a familiar figure with but a glance.
Why'd you stop, Enya?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: ...Is that Elder Adso?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: Who?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I didn't think... I would run into you here.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Because you should not be here, Saintess.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You know, Elder, when you're talking with the Saintess, you should be more—
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: I know, and yet I also know this is not the road she is meant to be on.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...What if it is to search for Kjeragandr?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: All shall seek Her in their time of need.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: So, what is it you need?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You instructed me to go on the Pilgrimage, so that I may atone for the sins of all Kjerag to Her. Thus, what I need is to find Her, and pray to Her for forgiveness.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I had wanted to get you out of the Vine-Bead Court at the time.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...And in doing so, the Court would not be 'hindered' by me.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I had hoped that while you were off on the Pilgrimage, we would be able to sway the Duke of Caster into handling Enciodes, and that when you returned, it would be to both parties worn and wary.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The Court would then take advantage of your return to declare a miracle, thus gaining true control.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But your brother declared war on the Court too soon, and I could not allow myself to fall into the hands of Caster or Enciodes, whether as their prisoner, or as their puppet.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I presume you being here means you are thinking of being like the Court clerics of old, going to Karlan to the ancient refuges in the legends, where you can wait for the moment your enemies are 'worn'.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: As such, there is no need for you to continue with your meaningless circuit, Saintess. Follow us to seek refuge atop the mountains.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: There will come a time that the people will beg on bended knee for you, and us, to appear.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The silent elders of the Court behind him stand up one after the other, forming a semicircle before the Saintess.
Joanna glances at Enya with unease, her hand subconsciously moving towards her crossbow, but Enya merely gives her a light shake of the head.
I can't stop. The next Catastrophe is nearly upon us.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What...?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The Saintess is right, Elder. You must have noticed the strange weather these past few days.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: We've already found an important clue about the 'Heart of Karlan'. We have the opportunity to learn the exact reason why a Catastrophe came to strike Kjerag.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Maybe even... the truth behind Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: ...It is meaningless.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Elder—
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Because Kjeragandr does not exist.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...What did you say?!
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: When I left the Court, you told me quite the opposite.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'Kjeragandr' can exist within the plants, the stones, our words and deeds, but you will not find Her true form atop this great land.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: She is not real, only existing within our imaginations.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Not even you can blaspheme against Her, Elder Adso!
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: ...When I was young, my faith in Her was much the same as yours. My father and uncle sent me to the Vine-Bead Court as a child, forcing me to secretly aid Clan Browntail with their mission, yet all I wanted to do was to serve Her in all sincerity.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: When the Great Elder tasked me with managing the Hall of Writings, I was giddy with glee. Every word to do with Her, whether they be fragmented pages or ridiculous essays, were studied by me over and over.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But the more you read, the more you realize that the so-called Kjeragandr is nothing more than a piece of 'scripture' assembled by mortal men.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Dare you deny it, Saintess? You too have seen what is said to be the original copy. It is no more than a few scant phrases, nothing like what we have today.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: It's not true, is it, Enya...?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: From marketplace chatter to historical records, even foreign legends from who-knows-where. Add cold gales and snowy peaks, and you have the 'miracles' of Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I persisted in my inspection of everything, hoping to find even the tiniest shred of evidence 'unlike the rest'. But all I found were more and more archetypical tales.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I even found signs of adaptation left behind by previous Court elders in the margins of a few books.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...Why didn't you tell anyone?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: And what would I say?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: What is Kjerag to you, Saintess?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: That is not a question any one child of Kjerag can answer.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Because Kjerag already has its answer. This is a nation of faith.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Enciodes has tried to undermine it, the Duke of Caster has tried to swallow it, but even they dare not defy Kjeragandr, at least not openly.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But if She doesn't exist, then who founded Kjerag?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: ...Our country was never one built atop Her flesh and bones. Rather, it was Her country that was built atop our flesh, our bones.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: It was the ancestors of the people of Kjerag who, amidst these snow-capped mountains of despair, labored tortuously to create a place where they could survive.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: People could never understand why fate decreed they should struggle in suffering, whilst others thrived in merriment.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: And so, abandoned upon these hopeless rocks, they had to seek consolation elsewhere, so that they may continue to live.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: From the cries for some almighty leader and the prayers for majesty and morality of every generation of Kjerag, 'Kjeragandr' was born.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: From the moment of Her birth, She became the foundation of Kjerag. Because of their need for faith, generation after generation must repeat a lie.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Kjerag needed virtue and law, needed art and aesthetics, needed family and tradition. With 'perfect timing', She would appear by our sides, leaving behind a few revelatory words of wisdom.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: From this came an unbreakable order, one that has lasted time immemorial.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: So in your eyes, this lie must be maintained at any cost, so that Kjerag itself can be maintained.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Indeed.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Every nation has its own lie. Kjerag's is 'Kjeragandr'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...I disagree.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: A lie can bring temporary reprieve, but it cannot become the eternal law through which we live.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You believe it is able to maintain order, so you care not for the sincere longing of the people. You treat their feelings as a tool you can control.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: When this longing tears through the veil you have constructed, the Vine-Bead Court and the faith it tends to shall be shattered beyond repair.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: From that point of view, you are your hated Enciodes's greatest ally.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: There is only one thing I wish to ask you. Do you believe Kjeragandr exists?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I... but...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: She sifts through her mind, through all her memories of that head maid... but she has already forgotten. How she looked, vague wisps of emotion, all forgotten.
There is no memory, no person who can vouch for her, no-one who can offer her words of comfort. Her heart lies empty...
...I do.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You claim this is all a lie, Elder Adso, which is proof that there is a truth to correspond with this lie.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: And so I believe in the passion and goodness preached by the scriptures. I believe Kjeragandr exists in Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Besides, I have already found the path to Her.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: If you must refute me, then come witness alongside me.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Your 'faith' is pointless, Saintess. Even greater disaster awaits Kjerag.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Enciodes's short-sightedness has already left her faith on the brink of collapse. This is our last chance to save her.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Ever since the Pilgrimage began, you have treated me as part of your conspiracy.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Because we have both dedicated our entire lives to the faith. It is impossible for you to not understand my devotion.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Then what is it you wish me to do?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: At a suitable hour, return with me to the Vine-Bead Court to regain control.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: We shall have to look at our specific circumstances to deal with the Duke of Caster, but Enciodes must be eliminated.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You are a kind soul, and cannot bear to harm your brother. I understand.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Enciodes must leave Kjerag. That is the most we can allow him.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: This is pure coercion.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: In return, I can take you to the place you seek.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: When I was young, I had searched all of Karlan. Since you must face the truth, this will be the quickest way to do it.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: ...I accept.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Enya?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: But on one condition. If we really do find proof of Kjeragandr, you shall renounce all your schemes regarding Enciodes.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: ...Very well.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'Kjeragandr' above, witness our vows.
attributes.name: Adso
